name: SOL 4h Value Reclaim Scale-In v1
edge_mechanism: failed downside auction reclaims value, confirmation improves across bars, and the position scales in only after structure proves the reclaim is holding
thesis: SOL 4h sits in mixed chop/transition rather than clean trend. That makes raw continuation fragile, but a failed breakdown that reclaims value and then proves hold quality over multiple bars can still create tradable rotations. The edge is not immediate catch-the-knife timing; it is delayed participation after value recovery begins to stabilize.
invalidation_condition: If reclaim events remain too sparse, if the second-bar confirmation never arrives often enough, or if reclaimed value is repeatedly lost before price can rotate materially, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Price flushes below short-term value, reclaims it, then stabilizes for one more bar so improved confirmation justifies adding rather than going all-in immediately.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In choppy 4h structure, failed breaks often mean-revert once late downside positioning is trapped. Delaying the full size until reclaim quality improves should reduce bad knife-catches without requiring ceremonial confirmation chains.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: SOL 4h is the best fit because it has enough amplitude for post-reclaim rotations to clear costs, while still producing more structure than TAO for a scale-in approach.

indicator_role_map.EMA_20: first confirmation that price is back above fast structure
indicator_role_map.EMA_55: rotation target and remaining-position management anchor
indicator_role_map.RSI_14: stretch qualifier so we only engage after real inventory displacement
indicator_role_map.ADX_14: regime veto against mature trend expansion

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The market may reclaim value briefly and then continue through it, turning the improved-confirmation add into a larger loss rather than a better entry.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If density is adequate but reclaim quality is poor, reduce the add-on requirement to a smaller structure hold instead of widening the entire setup.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: First entry captures the initial reversal, add-on improves exposure only on stronger holds, and most profits come from rotation back toward medium balance rather than long persistent trends.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: Screen shows non-sparse reclaim participation, the second-step add improves average trade quality, and the same behavior appears on ETH 4h or SOL 1h with different density.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: 0 trades, sub-10 trade screen output, or reclaim entries that repeatedly fail before the first scale-out target.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: low-to-moderate; reclaim, stretch, and ADX veto are compatible, while the add-on uses sequential confirmation rather than a separate rare event
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: controlled; the trigger is a flush-and-reclaim rather than a multi-bar breakout ceremony

validation_basket.why_included: Best lane for testing a value-reclaim scale-in structure under mixed chop and transition.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL 4h produces enough reclaim events and the add-on improves trade quality instead of just adding heat.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: SOL 4h reclaims are too sparse or too unstable after the first bar.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Regular failed-break reclaims with decent travel back toward EMA_55 and better expectancy on the added tranche.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the same reclaim-and-improve logic survives on a cleaner major with stronger chop reading.
validation_basket.continue_if: ETH 4h preserves the same reclaim pattern with slightly cleaner execution.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h loses the mechanism or becomes too inert.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Lower amplitude than SOL but cleaner reclaim holds.

validation_basket.why_included: Checks whether the mechanism densifies naturally on 1h without losing its structure.
validation_basket.continue_if: SOL 1h delivers healthier trade count with the same reclaim-and-hold sequence.
validation_basket.rotate_away_if: SOL 1h becomes noisy and invalidates the scale-in logic.
validation_basket.expected_if_right: Higher count, smaller rotations, same two-step confirmation behavior.

long: Add 40% when next bar closes >= EMA_20 and stays above VWAP
short: Add 40% when next bar closes <= EMA_20 and stays below VWAP

long: Initial stop loss: 0.90 * ATR_14 below first entry
Exit remaining 30% on touch of EMA_55 or 5 bars
short: Initial stop loss: 0.90 * ATR_14 above first entry
Exit remaining 30% on touch of EMA_55 or 5 bars
